title: Sri Lanka and the IMF reached a staff-level agreement on the fifth and sixth reviews of the four-year Extended Fund Facility
body: The deal unlocked roughly $700m 💰, lifting cumulative program disbursements to about $2.4bn, pending Executive Board approval. Authorities recorded low-single-digit inflation, stronger revenue, rebuilt reserves, and continued debt-restructuring progress, while being urged to keep fiscal discipline and accelerate structural reforms to cement a fragile recovery.

title: Colombo Stock Exchange rallied after a two-week ceasefire was announced in the Middle East
body: The bourse surged 4.21% to 21,917.60 on its broad gauge, while the blue-chip index climbed 4.42% to 6,090.50, with turnover at Rs. 6.61 billion and 11 crossings recorded. Gains were led by capital goods, banks and diversified groups, and the rupee strengthened modestly in spot trading as bond yields eased.

title: A day later, local equities turned volatile after large-scale air strikes in Lebanon
body: The market slipped by 73.06 points on the all-share benchmark as turnover slowed to Rs. 2.96 billion, even as the large-cap index eked out a modest rise with help from selective crossings. Traders also weighed the presence of an international lender’s review team, which supported sentiment despite elevated headline risk.

title: Parliament scrutinised a Rs. 13.2 billion fraud reported at National Development Bank
body: Lawmakers pressed regulators on why initially disclosed losses mushroomed within days, while supervisors imposed a dividend freeze, curtailed discretionary spending and branch expansion, and required an independent forensic audit. Ministers confirmed the head of state had been briefed and pledged measures to safeguard sector discipline as committee hearings gathered pace.

title: Government launched a Rs. 2.16 billion modernisation of Colombo Fort Railway Station with 15‑month completion target
body: Planned works include automated ticketing, lifts and overhead bridges for accessibility, upgraded sanitation, and new passenger-safety facilities under an ADB-supported efficiency project. The ceremony, attended by cabinet and parliamentary leaders, set out a shift to passenger‑centric design to serve hundreds of thousands of daily commuters.

title: Train services on the Northern Line resumed after India-funded repairs to cyclone-damaged sections
body: Restoration was financed via a $5 million grant drawn from a broader $450 million package and completed ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year. The reopening reestablished connectivity across impacted districts, easing passenger flows and freight movement after disruption from Cyclone Ditwah.

title: Cabinet approved the appointment of H. M. J. M. Herath as Commissioner General of Registration of Persons
body: The senior administrative officer, serving as Kegalle’s Government Agent, was tapped to fill a vacancy that had been covered in an acting capacity by a ministry official. The move aimed to accelerate rollout of the digital identity card and stabilise day‑to‑day operations; the nomination was tabled by the minister responsible for the digital economy.

title: Parliamentary Select Committee examined legal options to end the Provincial Council election impasse
body: Officials told members that incomplete delimitation under the 2017 mixed system left no operative framework to conduct polls, noting a review report was never finalised. Alternatives under study include repealing the amendment to reinstate the prior law, incorporating stronger provisions for women’s and youth representation, and seeking Attorney General guidance on Supreme Court rulings before summoning the Delimitation Commission.

title: Energy transition gathered pace as Hayleys completed an 800 kW rooftop solar project for LSEG Sri Lanka
body: The six‑building installation of 1,412 panels is expected to generate roughly 1,250 MWh annually, meeting close to 80% of daytime demand and avoiding about 850 tonnes of CO₂ per year ⚡. Engineers delivered the project within two months with zero accidents, including complex underground cabling across an active corporate site.

title: Commercial Bank and NCG Express rolled out fully digital bus ticketing across routes in a large private operator’s fleet
body: Conductors began using handheld devices to accept card taps, mobile wallets and QR scans, with instant settlement through an acquiring network and printed confirmations. The rollout, aligned with the national digital ticketing initiative, reduced cash handling, improved auditability and offered passengers faster, exact‑fare payments, with initial deployments live and a phased expansion planned.
